children.heading: Economic loss
children.label: 1 Economic loss
children.text: The term “economic loss” means any pecuniary loss resulting from harm (including the loss of earnings or other benefits related to employment, medical expense loss, replacement services loss, loss due to death, burial costs, and loss of business or employment opportunities) to the extent recovery for such loss is allowed under applicable State law.

children.heading: Harm
children.label: 2 Harm
children.text: The term “harm” includes physical, nonphysical, economic, and noneconomic losses.

children.heading: Noneconomic losses
children.label: 3 Noneconomic losses
children.text: The term “noneconomic losses” means losses for physical and emotional pain, suffering, inconvenience, physical impairment, mental anguish, disfigurement, loss of enjoyment of life, loss of society and companionship, loss of consortium (other than loss of domestic service), hedonic damages, injury to reputation and all other nonpecuniary losses of any kind or nature.

children.label: A
children.text: any organization which is described in and exempt from tax under section 501(a) of such title and which does not practice any action which constitutes a hate crime referred to in subsection (b)(1) of the first section of the Hate Crime Statistics Act ( note); orsection 501(c)(3) of title 2628 U.S.C. 5341See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: any not-for-profit organization which is organized and conducted for public benefit and operated primarily for charitable, civic, educational, religious, welfare, or health purposes and which does not practice any action which constitutes a hate crime referred to in subsection (b)(1) of the first section of the Hate Crime Statistics Act ( note).28 U.S.C. 5341

children.heading: State
children.label: 5 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Northern Mariana Islands, any other territory or possession of the United States, or any political subdivision of any such State, territory, or possession.

children.label: A
children.text: compensation (other than reasonable reimbursement or allowance for expenses actually incurred); or

children.label: B
children.text: any other thing of value in lieu of compensation,
